result.status_detail: h2_end_stream=2 to 1 fix did NOT resolve the envelope delivery issue. 4 chat?/api/v2/chat requests all blocked with CORS error and 0.0 kB size each. Times: 2.27s / 932ms / 949ms / 320ms. Chrome DevTools Response tab shows identical finding as #527: 'Failed to load response data - No data found for resource with given identifier' when clicking on the blocked chat request. qianwen chat area shows generic error message instead of APF warning. Retry logic fired 4 times but all 4 were blocked. URL: qianwen.com/chat/f6f594704dc84706975df99be47b580a. Nonces: sctld0wunq0, uqlfomjgoij, ges7fekj7j5, 909vx2bs4vn.
result.outcome: Browser still reports zero-byte response body. The fix to h2_end_stream flag alone is insufficient. APF must also emit proper HTTP/2 HEADERS frame BEFORE the DATA frame - specifically: :status=200, content-type=text/event-stream, access-control-allow-origin matching the request Origin. Without correct headers on the way out, Chrome will not buffer the body regardless of END_STREAM timing.

network_evidence.response_tab: Failed to load response data - No data found for resource with given identifier (same as #527)

notes: The #527 diagnostic remains valid: APF must emit HEADERS frame with :status=200 and valid CORS + content-type headers before DATA frame. Changing h2_end_stream from 2 to 1 fixes the END_STREAM flag placement on the DATA frame but doesn't fix the missing/invalid HEADERS frame. Chrome discards the response body because the preflight/headers validation fails before the body is buffered. Recommend: (1) confirm APF emits HTTP/2 HEADERS frame with proper pseudo-headers (:status=200) and regular headers (content-type, CORS), (2) once HEADERS is correct, the h2_end_stream=1 on DATA should then allow the body to be delivered, (3) alternatively switch strategy to inject warning as a valid qianwen SSE response that qianwen frontend renders as normal AI content (content in multi_load/iframe message per #521 template).
